cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. First, I need to recall what the passage said about the 1974 World Bank study. The passage states that the study concluded "more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." Wait, so the study is saying that after decades of growth, only a third of the population really benefited. That means the majority (which would be two-thirds) didn't get much benefit. But the options here are about what the economic growth brought. Looking at the options: (A) benefit to a third of their population (B) benefit to two thirds of their population (C) little benefit to their people (D) no benefit at all to their people The passage says "little benefit to a third of their population".
